text.title: Take-Home Rubric Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of take-home-rubric-template enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every criterion has explicit weights summing to exactly 100 — no implicit equal-weighting.
rule.rationale: Explicit weights surface trade-offs and force the hiring manager to name what matters most.

rule.statement: Each criterion ships with band anchors for at least 3 bands (e.g. exceeds / meets / below).
rule.rationale: Band anchors make scoring deterministic across reviewers; without them, scores drift toward 3/5.

rule.statement: Before live use the rubric is calibrated on at least 1 known-good submission with all reviewers in the room.
rule.rationale: Calibration converges reviewers' interpretation of anchors; the first live use is otherwise the calibration round.

rule.statement: The rubric carries an explicit numeric hire/no-hire threshold — not 'team feel'.
rule.rationale: A written threshold removes the lawyer's-job of relitigating each candidate; it also surfaces drift over time.

rule.statement: The rubric is versioned and owner-signed; changes between candidates require a written rationale.
rule.rationale: Mid-funnel rubric edits are a fairness risk; versioning + sign-off creates an audit trail.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
